title: ACME Software: public comps dashboard
subtitle: Synthetic strict-mode example for the Public Equity Investing dashboard pack.

metadata.source_posture: Synthetic provider export plus company filing example
metadata.decision_context: Whether ACME deserves a premium to vertical software peers.

hero.headline: ACME screens at a premium to slower-growth vertical software peers
hero.dek: The dashboard preserves peer logic, selected-multiple rationale, and source gaps.
hero.callout: The premium only works if ACME's retention and margin path remain above the peer median.

snapshot.label: Comps Posture
snapshot.detail: Selected peer set requires live market refresh before decision use.

snapshot.label: Selected Range
snapshot.value: Eight to ten times NTM revenue
snapshot.detail: Illustrative range based on the synthetic provider export.

data.label: PM read
data.summary: ACME merits a modest premium only if retention and margin evidence survive a live data refresh.
data.thesis_change: Peer set looks usable, but market data freshness is the gating item.
data.next_catalyst: Refresh provider export before using the valuation range.

items.title: Peer logic
items.body: Closest peers share vertical software exposure, recurring revenue, and similar enterprise buyer cycles.

items.title: Main caveat
items.body: Live share price, net debt, and consensus estimates need refresh before the dashboard is decision-grade.

cases.label: Downside
cases.summary: Premium compresses if retention fades or margin expansion stalls.

cases.label: Base
cases.summary: ACME holds a modest premium to slower-growth peers.

cases.label: Upside
cases.summary: Premium expands if retention and margin evidence both improve.

items: Live price, share count, net debt, and consensus export are required before decision use.
Peer set should be rechecked against business-model and liquidity filters.
